evidence: The word 'base' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it is physically tangible. It also connects to functional value (7) due to its foundational utility. Essential resonance (5) reflects its core role in being 'low.' Scores are minimized elsewhere to adhere to zero-count requirements.

Bilge strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical ship part) and functional utility (structural necessity). It weakly connects to epistemological identification/perception (named/observed part), scale.individual (single ship entity), and structural resonance (design pattern). Scores remain low elsewhere due to its specific physical context and lack of temporal or abstract implications.

The verb 'bilge' (in the PRP.low context) strongly resonates with physical sagging or lowering (ontological.material), affecting structural integrity (ontological.structural) at an individual scale (scale.individual). Its contingent nature (lawfulness.contingent) and perceptible effects (epistemological.perception) align with material and sensory aspects of shortness. Scores above 2 reflect direct connections to physical manifestation, while others remain minimal.

The word 'bottom' strongly resonates with ontological structural (base structure) and essential (core essence of 'low'). It connects to scale.individual (personal low status), epistemological identification (naming the low), and symmetry.destructive (negative aspect of low). Functional value (practical base) and cyclical symmetry (part of up/down cycles) also score moderately.

The word 'bottom' (v) in the PRP.low context strongly resonates with the material layer (physical action of lowering) and functional value (practical utility). It also connects weakly to perception (direct observation of lowering), experiential action (embodied doing), and the essential resonance of being low. Scores are otherwise minimal due to the verb's focused physical meaning.

Chunky strongly resonates with ontological.material (8) as it directly describes physical texture. It scores 3 in epistemological.perception due to sensory discernment and 4 in resonance.superficial because the attribute is surface-level. Aesthetic value (3) reflects its use in describing visually/texturally pleasing forms. Lower scores reflect weak connections to other slots in the PRP.low context.

Scores peak in ontological.material (8) as 'compact' directly describes physical form. Value_generation.functional (8) reflects practical utility of compactness. Resonance.essential (7) captures its core essence as a fundamental spatial quality. Epistemological.perception (7) ties to direct sensory observation of short/low height.

The word 'David Low' as a proper noun directly identifies an individual (scale.individual) with the physical attribute of being low (ontological.material). The name's identification (epistemological.identification) encapsulates this trait's essence (resonance.essential), and its predictability (lawfulness.predictable) stems from the name being tied to the PRP.low property.

The highest scores reflect the physical nature of a depression (ontological.material), its transformative emergence (temporal.transformation), and its capacity to generate functional value (value_generation.functional). Lower scores highlight contingent environmental influences (lawfulness.contingent) and catalytic effects like water accumulation (interconnection.catalytic).

The word 'dumpy' strongly resonates with physical, material aspects (ontological.material) and direct perception (epistemological.perception). It exists independently (interconnection.independent) and reflects an essential core trait (resonance.essential). Its predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable) aligns with its stable, short form.

The term 'half-length' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it directly describes a physical attribute. Epistemological.perception (8) reflects its sensory observability. Lawfulness.predictable (5) underscores its common usage. Lower scores reflect weak but real connections to individual scale (2), identification (2), independence (1), superficial resonance (2), functional value (3), and aesthetic value (1).

Heavyset strongly resonates with material and individual scale due to its physical, tangible nature. Perception and identification scores reflect direct sensory recognition of body type. Essential resonance captures its core essence as a robust, short stature. Predictable lawfulness aligns with its stable physical manifestation.

The word 'heel' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as a physical body part. Scores in relational/structural slots reflect its role in bodily structure. Epistemological identification/perception highlight its tangible nature. Symmetry.destructive and functional value link to potential negative aspects of being low.

The verb 'heel' in the context of PRP.low (shortness) resonates most strongly with material actions (e.g., pressing down to keep something low), individual-scale operations, and functional utility. Scores reflect its tangible, localized application and superficial resonance with maintaining low states.

The word 'little' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material) and direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception). It connects moderately to individual scale (scale.individual), independent existence (interconnection.independent), and predictable stability (lawfulness.predictable). The resonance.essential score reflects its core role in describing physical shortness, while superficial traits (resonance.superficial) and functional utility (value_generation.functional) also align with the atom's definition.

The word 'low' strongly resonates with physical perception (epistemological.perception 8), material existence (ontological.material 10), and independence as an inherent quality (interconnection.independent 8). It scores highly in predictability (lawfulness.predictable 8) and functional utility (value_generation.functional 7). Resonance.essential (7) reflects its core essence as a fundamental property, while comprehension (experience.comprehension 5) and identification (epistemological.identification 5) tie to understanding and naming the attribute.

The word 'low-set' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 10) as it directly describes physical structure. It has aesthetic value (value.aesthetic 7) in design contexts and functional utility (value.functional 5). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential 7) reflects core physical traits, while its predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable 7) aligns with consistent physical attributes.

The word 'pint-size' strongly resonates with physical (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic). The word 'pint-sized' strongly resonates with physical smallness (ontological.material), direct sensory observation (epistemological.perception), and its predictable usage (lawfulness.predictable). It also connects to semantic meaning (conveying size), individual scale (scale.individual), and essential resonance (core essence of being short).

Lower scores reflect its sensory observability (epistemological.perception 3) and lack of systemic or existential depth.

The term 'sawn-off' strongly resonates with physical/material aspects (ontological.material), individual-scale modifications (scale.individual), and superficial physical changes (resonance.superficial). The word 'short' in this context strongly resonates with physical material existence (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), direct sensory perception and identification (epistemological), independence from other factors (interconnection.independent), essential resonance (resonance.essential), and predictable stability (lawfulness.predictable). These scores reflect its core physical attribute and straightforward, stable nature.

The word 'short' strongly resonates with physical materiality (ontological.material) and sensory perception (epistemological.perception). It is a predictable, straightforward attribute (lawfulness.predictable) tied to individual entities (scale.individual). Scores for resonance.superficial and functional utility reflect its surface-level, practical nature.

The act of short-changing strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (8) due to its inherently harmful nature. Ethical value (7) arises from its moral violation. Relational ontology (5) reflects the interpersonal dynamics between cheater and victim. Lower scores reflect its localized, individual-scale impact and lack of systemic or existential resonance.

The word strongly resonates with physical material existence (ontological.material 10), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 8), and categorization (epistemological.identification 7). It aligns with predictability (a stable, expected state) and independent existence (not requiring other entities). Lower scores reflect its lack of deeper existential resonance or ethical implications.

The surname 'Low' directly aligns with the PRP.low attribute of 'not extending upward.' This creates a strong semantic resonance (resonance.essential) and ties the name to its individual existence (scale.individual), informational representation (ontological.informational), and functional use in contexts requiring a 'low' reference (value_generation.functional). The name's predictability (lawfulness.predictable) and comprehension of its meaning (experience.comprehension) further reinforce these scores.

The word resonates most strongly with ontological.material (physical stature) and epistemological.perception (direct observation of shortness).
